articles.title: Nico Williams: alarm at Athletic, World Cup doubt
articles.description: Concern has taken hold within Athletic Club and the Spanish national team. Everything points to Nico Williams being unable to pull on his boots for the three matches Bilbao still have left this season. An untimely muscle injury in yesterday’s match against Valencia appears to have abruptly halted the winger’s progress right at the decisive stage of the campaign. The forward, a key figure in Ernesto Valverde’s system, went off with discomfort this Sunday. Although the club has not issued a definitive medical report with a recovery timetable, the feeling inside the dressing room is pessimistic regarding his immediate involvement with the Lions. This situation not only affects Athletic’s interests, as they fight to secure their European place, but also extends to the international stage. Nico Williams is one of Luis de la Fuente’s regular call-ups, and his presence at the upcoming World Cup is now in serious doubt. The player has already begun his recovery process with the club’s physiotherapists. The main priority is to avoid a relapse that would definitively rule him out of the biggest tournament in the world. The winger is "keeping his fingers crossed" that the timeline works in his favour and that the injury is not serious. This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇪🇸 here.

articles.title: Last two hantavirus evacuation flights to depart Monday afternoon
articles.description: STORY: :: Granadilla de Abona, Spain / May 10, 2026 :: Spain's health minister says the last two hantavirus evacuation flights will depart Monday afternoon :: Monica Garcia, Spanish health minister “For tomorrow, at 07:00 the ship will refuel. Refueling will take a few hours, followed by an hour of provisioning. Later in the afternoon, there are still two flights scheduled: the Australia flight, carrying six people, and the Netherlands flight, which is expected to carry 18 people.” :: Garcia added that protocols must be followed after a French passenger developed symptoms “One of the people traveling on the flight from France showed symptoms and, as you know, this is something that may be expected at some point—some passengers may develop symptoms. Since we have already identified symptoms in one of the people who had prior contact, what must be followed are the protocols. The French health authorities will continue to develop the different protocols.” One flight from Australia will carry six passengers and another from the Netherlands will take 18 passengers, Minister Monica Garcia told journalists during a press conference in the port of Granadilla de Abona where MV Hondius cruise ship is docked. A French passenger who developed symptoms consistent with the hantavirus infection on the repatriation flight on Sunday afternoon was quarantined in a hospital in Paris and is being tested, with results expected later on Monday, the minister said. "The French experts will do all that is needed to manage this. As I've said many times, this is not COVID. It doesn't mean there is no issue, but it means we can handle it," WHO Director General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus added.

articles.title: NBA playoffs: Record-breaking Knicks sweep 76ers as New York fans take over Philadelphia
articles.description: Knicks return to East finals after 4-0 series win Team hit record 11 three-pointers in first quarter Knicks fans dominate in 76ers’ home arena The New York Knicks are back in the Eastern Conference finals, setting an NBA postseason record with 11 three-pointers in the first quarter in front of a raucous crowd mostly rooting for the road team in Philadelphia. The Knicks’ 144-114 win on Sunday completed their series sweep of the 76ers. Deuce McBride hit seven of New York’s NBA postseason record-tying 25 three-pointers and scored 25 points. Jalen Brunson had 22 points and Josh Hart and Karl-Anthony Towns each scored 17 in the Knicks’ latest lopsided playoff victory. Continue reading...
